D2 Vision L3 Apply Reference

White Paper in Shadow

slide the shadow deeper and watch white paper stay white even as it reflects less light than nearby grey

What looks white is the surface judged to reflect the most light in its region — not the surface sending the most light to the eye. The brain factors out the shadow.

Slide the shadow deeper until the white card in shade sends the eye exactly as much light as the grey card in sunlight. Toggle the meter to confirm the two equal luminances — they still look white and grey.
